page: Microsoft Azure D96als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes scoring in the top 10 percent, while large-block uncached reads rank in the top 25 percent. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform exceptionally well, with single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier throughput. Additionally, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions, which assist in accelerating low-latency machine learning inference, as demonstrated by strong performance in small and medium LLM text generation benchmarks.

Given its high compute density relative to memory capacity, the D96als_v7 provides a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The lack of bundled local SSDs and public IPv4 addresses represents a tradeoff that reduces baseline infrastructure costs. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, batch processing, static web serving, Redis key-value databases, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. Lacking bundled local storage and GPUs, this instance is optimized for raw CPU throughput. Benchmark data places its single-core and multi-core performance in the top 10 percent, with exceptional results in cryptographic operations, data compression, and static web serving. The processor supports AVX-512 and VNNI, enabling efficient execution of vector calculations and small-to-medium scale LLM inference. This server is highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not demand high memory-to-core ratios, making it an excellent fit for batch processing, distributed compute tasks, Redis deployments, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96als_v7 is a compute-focused instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for intensive batch processing and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96als_v7 features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for compute-heavy and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and top-tier processing performance for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs with 192.0 GB of RAM
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
AVX-512 and VNNI support for accelerated vector and inference tasks
No bundled local storage, optimized for external premium storage
Best suited for compute-intensive batch processing, Redis, and web serving
